description: A targeted flow for users experiencing back pain, focusing on mattress support and spinal alignment.

headerContent.headline: End Back Pain for Good
headerContent.subheadline: Hey, I'm Ashley — and I specialize in helping people wake up without that nagging back pain. If you've been waking up stiff, sore, or feeling like you barely slept — you're in the right place.
headerContent.avatarIntroScript: Back pain is one of the most common reasons people don't sleep well. And here's the thing most people don't realize: your mattress could be making it worse. Let me ask you a few quick questions to understand what's going on.

questionContent.questionText: Where do you feel pain when you wake up?

answerOptions.label: Lower back
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Lower back pain is incredibly common — and often caused by a mattress that doesn't support your spine's natural curve.

answerOptions.label: Upper back / shoulders
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Upper back and shoulder pain often comes from pressure points. Your mattress might be too firm — or too soft.

answerOptions.label: Neck
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Neck pain can really disrupt your day. This is usually about pillow height AND mattress support working together.

answerOptions.label: Multiple areas
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Pain in multiple areas suggests your body isn't getting the support it needs overall. Good news — that's exactly what we can fix.

questionContent.questionText: How would you rate your morning pain?

answerOptions.label: Mild — goes away quickly
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Even mild pain that goes away is a sign something's off. The goal is waking up feeling great — not just 'okay after coffee.'

answerOptions.label: Moderate — lingers for hours
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Pain that lingers for hours? That's your body telling you something needs to change. You shouldn't have to 'warm up' just to feel normal.

answerOptions.label: Severe — affects my whole day
answerOptions.avatarResponse: I'm sorry you're dealing with that. When pain affects your whole day, getting the right support at night becomes even more critical.

questionContent.questionText: How old is your current mattress?

answerOptions.label: Less than 3 years
answerOptions.avatarResponse: A newer mattress that's causing pain? That usually means it's not the right fit for your body type or sleep style.

answerOptions.label: 3-7 years
answerOptions.avatarResponse: At 3-7 years, a mattress might look fine but could be losing the support your back needs. The materials break down before you see it.

answerOptions.label: 7-10 years
answerOptions.avatarResponse: 7-10 years is right when most mattresses start failing. Your body has probably changed in that time too — but your mattress hasn't adapted.

answerOptions.label: Over 10 years
answerOptions.avatarResponse: Over 10 years? Your mattress has done its job — but it's time for an upgrade. You deserve to wake up without pain.

avatarContent.scriptText: Here's what I'm seeing: you're dealing with real pain, and your current sleep setup isn't helping. The good news? This is fixable. Tempur-Pedic mattresses are specifically designed to support your spine's natural alignment while relieving pressure points. That means less tossing and turning, and waking up actually feeling rested — not broken. Based on your answers, I've picked out mattresses that are proven to help with back pain. Ready to see them?

ctaContent.headline: Your Back Pain Solutions
ctaContent.bodyText: These Tempur-Pedic mattresses are specifically designed for spinal support and pressure relief. Thousands of customers with back pain have found relief with these options.
